feed.title: Hawaiʻi County Bill Tracker — all updates

entry.title: New: Bill 176 (Hawaiʻi County) — On agenda 2026-07-22
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 16, ARTICLE 1, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE GENERAL PLAN

Establishes amendment procedures for the General Plan, including a comprehensive review every ten years and interim amendments; describes powers and duties of the planning director and planning commissions.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-22

View on the council site

entry.title: New: Bill 175 (Hawaiʻi County) — On agenda 2026-07-22
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-50, AS AMENDED, AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER 6 OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO TRANSIENT ACCOMMODATIONS

Changes certain responsibilities for proposed vacation rental laws under Ordinance No. 25-50 from the finance director to the planning director; exempts prospective vacation rentals that have submitted a valid use permit or special permit application from Ordinance No. 25-50

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-22

View on the council site

entry.title: New: Bill 174 (Hawaiʻi County) — On agenda 2026-07-22
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 26-44, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2027

Appropriates revenues in the revenues in the State Grants – Kona Low Small Business Recovery Grant account ($500,000) and appropriates the same to the Kona Low Small Business Recovery Grant Program account. Funds will be used to support one-time relief to eligible Hawaiʻi Island small businesses that sustained material and economic or physical damage from the Kona Low Storm.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-22

View on the council site

entry.title: New: Bill 173 (Hawaiʻi County) — On agenda 2026-07-22
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 19 OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A BED AND BREAKFAST HOME CLASSIFICATION FOR REAL PROPERTY TAXES

Establishes a new “bed and breakfast home” property tax class for any property that is a vacation rental and the owner’s principal residence; establishes a three percent annual assessment increase cap for the new tax class

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-22

View on the council site

entry.title: New: Bill 172 (Hawaiʻi County) — On agenda 2026-07-22
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 26-44, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2027

Appropriates revenues in the revenues in the State Motor Vehicle Weight Tax account ($6,282.72) and appropriates the same to the Vehicle Registration Other Current Expenses account. Funds will be used for expenses associated with programming and updating the County’s motor vehicle registration system.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-22

View on the council site

entry.title: New: Bill 171 (Hawaiʻi County) — On agenda 2026-07-22
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 4, SECTION 2-12.7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO FISCAL IMPACT STATEMENTS

Requires that a fiscal impact statement prepared by the finance director be submitted with Council bills that have fiscal implications.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-22

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 168 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed First Reading → —
entry.content:
REPEALS ORDINANCE NOS. 19-38, 09-50, 09-49, 06-138, 06-137, 95-118, 92-36, 90-10, 86-49, 533 (1980), AND 594 (1973) AND AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLE 8, SECTION 25-8-3 (NORTH KONA ZONING MAP) AND SECTION 25-8-5 (KAILUA URBAN ZONING MAP), OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM VILLAGE COMMERCIAL – 7,500 SQUARE FEET (CV-7.5) AND MULTIPLE

Director Initiated) (Area: 6,467 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation to revert the current zoning of 3.331 acres from Village Commercial to Double-Family Residential and 3.227 acres from Multiple-Family Residentia

Status: Passed First Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-07

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 170 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-07-07
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLE 1, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO ZONING

Distinguishes data centers from data processing facilities for zoning and land use purposes; defines “data center.”

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-07

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 169 (Hawaiʻi County): Postponed → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 42, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PUBLIC ACCESS, OPEN SPACE, AND NATURAL RESOURCES PRESERVATION

Requires that all qualifying lands ranked with a score of 70 percent or higher be included on the Public Access, Open Space, and Natural Resource Preservation Commission’s recommendation list to the mayor; requires the finance director to make reasonable efforts to acquire authorized properties within ten years for properties ranked 90 percent or higher and five years for properties ranked less than 90 percent; requires the finance director to reassess any such properties not acquired within the designated timeframes.

Status: Postponed → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-07

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 167 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – Justice Assistance Grant-Special Response Team Capability Enhancement Grant account ($35,023)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 166 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-50, AS AMENDED, AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER 6 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO TRANSIENT ACCOMMODATIONS

Delays the effective date of Ordinance No. 25-50, relating to transient accommodations, until September 1, 2026.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 162 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 3 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES

Adds a new article to establish signage requirements for paid parking facilities, to include a schedule of rates, notice of any additional fees, hours of operation, and information on towing procedures.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 163 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed First Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 19, ARTICLE 8, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXES

Establishes a new real property tax dedication for certain properties that are owned by a descendant of a person who owned the property at least one hundred years ago to be subject to the minimum tax.

Status: Passed First Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 165 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-07-08
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLE X OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO PUBLIC ACCESS, OPEN SPACE, AND NATURAL RESOURCES PRESERVATION

Increases the annual allocation dedicated to the Public Access, Open Space, and Natural Resources Preservation maintenance fund from 0.25 percent to 0.5 percent of real property tax revenue; removes the accrual limit for the fund; provides that all expenses from the fund must align with the fund’s purpose

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 164 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed First Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 13, DIVISION 2, SECTION 2-69, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE HOUSING ADMINISTRATOR

Requires Council confirmation for the Housing Administrator.

Status: Passed First Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 157 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 7 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO DISASTER RECOVERY

Establishes a County Disaster Recovery Program to coordinate short- and long-term recovery efforts following major disasters, led by a recovery officer appointed by the mayor with the responsibility for recovery planning, interagency coordination, community engagement, funding management, and the development of interim and long-term recovery and resilience plans.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 161 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Fund Balance From Previous Year - Reserved account ($443,775.57)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 160 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Real Property Tax Penalties account ($1,125,000)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 159 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-06-03
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLES III, V, VI, VII, VIII, AND X OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A BIENNIAL OPERATING BUDGET AND CAPITAL BUDGET

Establishes biennial budgeting for the County, with an initial primary budget to be adopted every odd-numbered year and a supplemental operating budget to be adopted every even-numbered year.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-03

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 158 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-06-03
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLES III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII, X, XIII, AND XVI, OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO THE STRUCTURE AND ORGANIZATION OF THE COUNTY

Converts the County’s government to a hybrid council-manager system, in which certain County departments and operations are overseen by an appointed County Manager and others are overseen by an elected Mayor.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-03

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 156 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 25, SECTION 2-137, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO APPROPRIATION OF FUNDS TO NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS

Requires a nonprofit organization applying for County grant funds to certify, under penalty of perjury, that the organization has internal policies relating to management, audits, fiscal procedures, nepotism, and conflicts of interest.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 155 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – HOME Investment Partnership- American Rescue Plan Program account ($1,977,568)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-20

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 152 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-06-17
entry.content:
INITIATES AN AMENDMENT TO ARTICLE III, SECTION 3-18, OF THE CHARTER OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I (2024 EDITION), RELATING TO THE OFFICE OF THE COUNTY AUDITOR

Clarifies that the County Auditor has the same power with respect to employees within the Office of the County Auditor as department heads in the Executive Branch and specifies that the County Auditor may appoint a deputy.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-17

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 153 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the State Grants – Coffee Berry Borer/Coffee Leaf Rust Pesticide Subsidy Program account ($150,000)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-03

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 154 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-05-04
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLES 1, 5, AND 7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO COMMERCIAL PARKING LOTS AND GARAGES

Removes commercial parking lots and garages as a permitted use in the general, village commercial, industrial-commercial mixed, limited industrial, general industrial, and Downtown Hilo commercial zoning districts.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-04

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 149 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Parks and Recreation Miscellaneous Contributions account ($160,000)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 151 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-46, AS AMENDED, RELATING TO PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2025, TO JUNE 30, 2026

Adds the Department of Public Works – Safe Routes to Waiākea School – Kīlauea Avenue Part 1 project ($1,386,656) to the Capital Budget. Funds for these project shall be provided from State Grants Receivable.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-20

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 150 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the State Grants – Safe Routes to School – Non- Infrastructure account ($50,344)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-20

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 148 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
DESIGNATES TAX MAP KEY: 6-6-001:010 AS “LAʻELAʻE PARK”, AMENDS CHAPTER 15, ARTICLE 8, SECTION 15-68.1, AND AMENDS CHAPTER 14, ARTICLE 1, SECTION 14-1 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PARKS AND RECREATIONAL FACILITIES

Adds Laʻelaʻe Park, located in Waimea, to the Parks and Recreational Facility Schedule and to Section 14-1 of the County Code, which prohibits the consumption of alcohol at any time.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-20

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 136 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
RELATES TO PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026, TO JUNE 30, 2027

Draft 3 requires a total appropriation of $380,200,000, of which $55,700,000 are intended to be funded by Federal Grants, $23,000,000 to be funded by State Grants, $292,500,000 to be funded in whole or part by bonds, $8,500,000 to be funded by private funds, and $500,000 to be funded by the Public Access, Open Space, and Natural Resources Preservation Maintenance Fund.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-04

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 135 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
ESTABLISHES AN OPERATING BUDGET FOR THE COUNTY OF HAWAI‘I FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2026, TO JUNE 30, 2027

Draft 2 includes estimated revenues of $976,408,620 and appropriations of $975,980,388.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-04

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 147 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-04-07
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2 AND CHAPTER 25 OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO TRANSIENT ACCOMMODATIONS

Establishes operational standards and use regulations for transient vacation rentals, which include hosted bed and breakfasts (B&Bs) and un-hosted short-term vacation rentals (STVRs); allows B&Bs as a permitted use in the single-family residential zoning district; allows B&Bs as a permitted use in areas within the residential agricultural, family agricultural, and agricultural zoning districts that are also classified as State land use urban or rural; allows STVRs as a permitted use in the entire multiple-family residential district and in certain coastal areas adjacent to General Plan-designa

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-07

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 146 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the State Grants – Transit-Oriented Development Capital Improvement Program for 34 Rainbow Affordable Housing Project account ($1,000,000)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 145 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – Housing Choice Voucher Program account ($1,000,000)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 144 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
ADOPTS A PAY PLAN FOR PERSONS EMPLOYED BY THE COUNTY OTHER THAN THOSE WHOSE PAY IS OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR, DESIGNATING PAYROLL PERIODS, PAY DAYS, PRESCRIBING REQUIREMENTS WITH RESPECT TO OFFICIAL FIDELITY BONDS, AND REPEALING ORDINANCE NO. 25-39 AND ALL AMENDMENTS THERETO, SAID ORDINANCE TO BE KNOWN AS THE SALARY ORDINANCE OF 2026

Establishes rates of compensation for appointed employees in the Legislative Branch and the Office of Housing and Community Development, the County Physician, Student Helpers, and Senior Community Services Aids for various County agencies.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 143 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Contribution from Private Sources – Research and Development Miscellaneous Private Contributions account ($12,500)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 131 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO NEIGHBORHOOD COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CN-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-2-034:045 (Applicant: CU

Hawaiʻi Federal Credit Union) (Area: 22,500 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to construct a 41-stall parking lot for the proposed new credit union facility to be located on Tax Map Key: 2-2-034:036. The property is located at 494 Hīnano Street.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 130 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 09-44, WHICH AMENDED SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2005 EDITION), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO NEIGHBORHOOD COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CN-10) AT WAIĀKEA HOUSELOTS, WAIĀKEA SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-

Hawaiʻi Federal Credit Union) (Area: 22,500 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this request to change of zone amendment for a ten-year extension to Condition C (Complete Construction), amendment to Condition D (Dedication of Road Widening), and deletion of Condition E (Roadway Improvements) which would allow the applicant to construct a new credit union branch facility. The property is located at 533 Manono Street. R

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 141 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the following accounts: Hale Kikaha Rental Income ($36,000), Department Charges ($110,000), and Sundry Revenues Current – Hale Kikaha ($18)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 140 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the following accounts: Housing Choice Voucher – Dolphin Bay ($23,912), Dolphin Bay Rental Income ($50,000), Department Charges ($176,610), and Sundry Revenues Current – Dolphin Bay ($8)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 139 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – Benefits Enrollment Center – National Council on Aging account ($150,000)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 137 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-22 (PUNA DISTRICT ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 20 ACRES (A-20a) TO FAMILY AGRICULTURAL – 1 ACRE (FA-1a) AT ʻŌLAʻA, PUNA, HAWAI‘I, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 1-7-016:010 (Applicant

Benjamin and Olimpia Love) (Area: 5.79 Acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide the property into five lots. The property is located at 17-4050 Kuaʻāina Road.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 138 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 15, ARTICLE 2, DIVISION 1, SECTION 15-20, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO COMMERCIAL ACTIVITY IN COUNTY PARK AREAS

Prohibits the issuance of permits, licenses, or other authorizations for commercial activity within Kohanaiki Beach Park.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 142 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 14 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY ADDING A NEW ARTICLE RELATING TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF AN ADOPT-A-COUNTY-STREET PROGRAM

Establishes an Adopt-A-County-Street program in partnership with the Department of Public Works to enable volunteers to assist in maintaining clean and safe County streets, roadways, and right-of-ways.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 134 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Housing Production, Program Income - Repayment account ($16,100)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 133 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the Federal Grants – Staffing for Adequate Fire and Emergency Response account ($271,447)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 132 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-05-19
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 6 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED) BY ADDING AN ARTICLE RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES IN KAILUA VILLAGE

Adds a new article to regulate parking rates at private parking facilities in Kailua Village.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-19

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 129 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 10, DIVISION 5, SECTION 24-280, SCHEDULE 28 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO NO PARKING AT ANYTIME

Prohibits parking at any time for certain portions of Ali‘i Drive fronting Kahalu‘u Beach Park.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 121 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 09-159, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM AGRICULTURAL – 5 ACRES (A-5a) TO MULTIPLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 30,000 SQUARE FEET (RM-30) AT KEAUHOU, NORTH KONA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 7-8-010:101 (Applicant

Kona Country Club, Inc.) (Area: 51.058 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested ten-year time extension to Condition D (complete construction). The property is located mauka of the Māmalahoa Highway Bypass, between the highway and the Kona Country Club mauka golf course.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 120 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 04-149, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM AGRICULTURAL – FIVE ACRES (A-5a) TO SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 20,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-20) AT KALAOA 1ST AND 2ND, NORTH KONA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 7-3-011:068 (Applicant

Ernest and Elizabeth Young) (Area: 1.86 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested deletion of Condition H, which prohibits construction of a second dwelling unit on the property, and to amend Condition C for a five-year time extension to secure final subdivision approval. The property is located at the southwest corner of the intersection of Mahilani Drive and Māmalahoa Highway.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 119 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 02-93, WHICH AMENDED SECTION 25-8-7 (NORTH AND SOUTH KOHALA DISTRICTS ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL (A-5a) TO RESIDENTIAL AND AGRICULTURAL (RA-2a) AT ‘ŌULI, SOUTH KOHALA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 6-2-007:002 (Applicant

Clemson and Janet Lam) (Area: 5 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested amendment to Condition D, which prohibits construction of a second dwelling unit on the property. The property is located at 62-2039 Kawaihae Road.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 127 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-04-23
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 5 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A CONSTRUCTION CODE COMMISSION

Establishes a construction code commission to advise the Mayor, County Council, and Director of Public Works on the adoption, updating, amendment, and interpretation of the County construction codes.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 128 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 19, ARTICLE 6, SECTION 19-46.1, AND ARTICLE 11, SECTION 19-90, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXES

Adds a new “Residential Tier Three” real property tax classification for certain properties with a net taxable real property value of more than $4,000,000.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-03-04

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 126 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed First Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLES 1, 3, 5, AND 7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO SPECIAL DISTRICTS

Creates the Downtown Pāhoa commercial district as a new special zoning district; establishes new permitted uses.

Status: Passed First Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 125 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed First Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLE 7, DIVISION 4, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE PĀHOA VILLAGE DESIGN DISTRICT

Clarifies duties, procedures, and other regulations regarding the Pāhoa Village Design Guidelines and the Pāhoa Design Review Committee; makes other technical and non-substantive amendments.

Status: Passed First Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 124 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-06-16
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-26 (PĀHOA ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED) BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM VILLAGE COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CV-10) AND SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO DOWNTOWN PĀHOA COMMERCIAL (DPC) AT WAIAKAHIʻULA, PUNA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY THE AREA AS MORE PARTICULARLY DESCRIBED HEREIN

Rezones certain areas in Pāhoa to a prospective special zoning district classification to be known as the Downtown Pāhoa Commercial District, which is proposed for establishment via

Latest action: On agenda 2026-06-16

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 123 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-7 (NORTH & SOUTH KOHALA DISTRICT ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 5 ACRES (A-5a) TO RESIDENTIAL AGRICULTURAL – 2.5 ACRES (RA-2.5a) AT WAIMEA, SOUTH KOHALA, HAWAI‘I, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 6-2-009:018 (Applicant

Joseph Gaglione) (Area: 6.063 Acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this amendment to the State Land Use Boundaries Map for the requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide his property into two lots. The property is located at 62-2239 Kānehoa Place.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-03-04

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 122 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS THE STATE LAND USE DISTRICT BOUNDARIES MAP WITHIN THE COUNTY BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM THE AGRICULTURAL DISTRICT TO THE RURAL DISTRICT AT WAIMEA, SOUTH KOHALA, HAWAI‘I, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 6-2-009:018 ; and

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-03-04

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 118 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the Federal Grants – Hawaiʻi County Police Department Pedestrian and Bicycle Safety Grant account ($54,098) and appropriates the same to the Hawaiʻi County Police Department Pedestrian and Bicycle Safety Program account.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 117 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the Federal Grants – High Intensity Impact Grant account ($340,000) and appropriates the same to the High Intensity Impact Grant Program account.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site
